title: General Audio Reel
role: Gameplay Audio Programmer, Technical Sound Designer, Composer
description: Over the past few years, I've had the opportunity to work on a wide variety of projects across different genres and platforms alongside many talented studios and creators. This reel showcases some of my favorite work in gameplay audio programming, technical sound design, and music composition.
platforms: PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Meta Quest 3S, Meta Quest 3, NEX Playground

title: Orcs Must Die! By The Blade
role: Technical Sound Designer, Gameplay Audio Programmer
description: Orcs Must Die! By the Blade was a VR project where I explored the unique possibilities of interactive audio. I developed gameplay-driven sound systems that reacted to player movement, weapon impact force, and combat interactions, creating a more immersive experience. It was one of those projects where programming became a powerful creative tool for sound design.

title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Rooftop Mayhem
description: On this project, I owned the sound design while collaborating closely with the composer and engineering team. As a lifelong fan of the franchise, it was incredibly rewarding to contribute to the TMNT universe by creating impactful gameplay and UI sounds. The classic arcade titles, especially Turtles in Time, served as the primary inspiration for the game's energetic audio style.

title: Killer Klowns From Outer Space
description: My primary responsibility was implementing the audio created by the sound design team. This involved building FMOD events, configuring parameters, and integrating them with Unreal Engine gameplay systems. Since Killer Klowns is an online asymmetric multiplayer game, many of the technical challenges revolved around network replication and multiplayer audio behavior, making it an excellent opportunity to solve complex implementation problems.

title: Al-Umbra
description: Al-Umbra is currently in development, where I lead the technical audio direction by creating music, sound effects, and gameplay audio systems in Unity. Using Wwise, we've built an immersive audio experience that takes full advantage of spatial audio, which has been particularly challenging and rewarding for a Metroidvania with highly interconnected environments.

title: NEKOME
description: Although my contribution to NEKOME focused primarily on technical audio implementation, it has been a great opportunity to collaborate with an experienced team and help integrate gameplay audio into the project. My work centered on building reliable audio systems while supporting the overall implementation pipeline.

title: The Shadow Syndicate
description: Creating the audio identity for a noir detective game starring anthropomorphic characters required extensive research and experimentation. Following a simple creative direction: keeping everything organic I designed and implemented gameplay sounds, developed MetaSound presets for spells, and created systems for environmental soundscapes that helped bring this unique world to life.

title: Beat the Heat!
description: For this project, I contributed additional music, created sound assets, and oversaw the overall audio direction. One of the most interesting systems dynamically changes the ambience based on the player's actions, allowing the environment to react naturally to resource management and reinforcing the game's core mechanics.

title: My Friend The Painter
description: Created during a Global Game Jam, this project embraced an artistic audio direction where every sound reinforced the visual style. Gameplay feedback relied on pizzicato string recordings, while the soundtrack featured an intimate solo piano score, creating a cohesive and expressive atmosphere.

title: La Casa de Papel
description: This project challenged us to translate the distinctive audio identity of Money Heist into an interactive mobile experience. I created and implemented the game's sound effects in Unreal Engine while working closely with composer Camilo Forero to develop a soundtrack that blended orchestral elements with modern synthesizers, preserving the spirit of the original series. The result was an audio experience that felt authentic while adapting naturally to gameplay.

title: Rompe!
description: I composed the original soundtrack for Rompe!, creating music that matched the game's playful and energetic personality. The score was designed to reinforce the pacing of the gameplay while complementing its colorful visual style.

title: Heroes del Bicentenario
description: I composed the original soundtrack and contributed additional sound design for this educational title. The music blends orchestral and traditional influences to support the historical setting while remaining engaging for younger audiences.

title: VOCES
description: For VOCES, I created both the sound design and original music, focusing on building an emotional atmosphere that supported the narrative. Every sound was carefully crafted to strengthen the player's connection with the story.

title: Garak & Bam
description: On Garak & Bam, I implemented the sound effects using FMOD, and developed gameplay audio systems in Unity with C#, I also had the opportunity to write some additional music. The project combined technical implementation with creative sound design to deliver responsive, satisfying gameplay feedback. This game is still a work in progress.

title: Graphite
description: I contributed additional sound design and served as an audio implementation consultant, helping refine gameplay feedback and ensuring that new audio assets integrated seamlessly into the existing Unreal Engine pipeline.
